description: Ion RangeAnalysis MObjectKeysLength::computeRange — Upper Bound Fixed at NativeObject::MAX_SLOTS_COUNT Ignores TypedArray Indexed-Property Keys (CVE-2024-29943, Pwn2Own 2024). CVE-2024-29943 (Pwn2Own 2024). MObjectKeysLength::computeRange in js/src/jit/RangeAnalysis.cpp:1824 incorrectly computes the range of Object.keys(x).length as [0, NativeObject::MAX_SLOTS_COUNT]. This range is too narrow: when x is a large TypedArray with named properties (e.g., Uint8Array(2**28-4) with .a through .f set), the actual keys count can exceed MAX_SLOTS_COUNT. The JIT's IonMonkey range analysis trusts this overly narrow range and uses it to eliminate bounds checks and optimize loop iteration. An attacker can train the JIT with a small object (few keys), then call the JIT-compiled code with a large TypedArray that has many more keys than the range claims. The incorrect range leads to out-of-bounds memory access. The fix (changeset 45d29e78c0d8) removes MObjectKeysLength::computeRange entirely and deletes the `compute_range: custom` declaration from MIROps.yaml, falling back to the default unconstrained range. Exploited at Pwn2Own 2024 by Manfred Paul for Firefox RCE.
